indoor_outdoor.label: Indoor vs. Outdoor
indoor_outdoor.tooltip: Where the fixture lives sets the baseline weather and ingress protection it must survive.
indoor_outdoor.learnMore: Indoor locations are typically Dry rated, however, you will be able to select Damp or Wet if needed for fixtures located in restrooms or similar. Outdoor locations always need at least Damp protection, and most need Wet / IP67+ because they face rain, sprinklers, snow, or wash-down.

environment_rating.label: Environment Rating
environment_rating.tooltip: How much moisture the location exposes the fixture to: Dry, Damp, or Wet.
environment_rating.learnMore: Dry = conditioned interiors, no condensation. Damp = covered exterior / bathrooms where moisture is present but not direct. Wet = direct water contact (rain, sprinklers, fountains). The rating drives which profiles, lenses, and tape are eligible.

wet_rating.label: Outdoor / Wet Rating
wet_rating.tooltip: Survives direct water contact such as rain, sprinklers, or wash-down.
wet_rating.learnMore: For outdoor installations, we recommend fully encapsulated IP67+ products that can survive rain and snow but are fully enclosed to also handle dirt and bugs.

ip_rating.label: IP Rating
ip_rating.tooltip: A two-digit code that rates how well an enclosure resists solids (dust, debris) and liquids (water).
ip_rating.learnMore: IP65 — Dust tight and protected against water jets, but not submersion. IP67 — Dust tight and can be temporarily submerged up to 1 meter for 30 minutes. IP68 — Dust tight and rated for continuous submersion beyond 1 meter at manufacturer-specified depth.

light_type.label: Light Type
light_type.tooltip: The color behavior of the LED: static white, tunable white, dim-to-warm, or full color.
light_type.learnMore: Static white = one fixed CCT (e.g., 3000K). Tunable white = blends between two CCTs (e.g., 1800K to 4000K). Dim-to-warm = warms as it dims (like halogen / incandescent). Full-color = RGB / RGBW and addressable pixels.

color_mode.label: Color Mode
color_mode.tooltip: How full-color tape is driven: one color for the whole run, or individually addressable pixels.
color_mode.learnMore: Analog RGB / RGBW drives the whole strip to a single color at once. Addressable pixel (SPI) controls each pixel for chases, gradients, and video effects — and requires a DMX/SPI controller.

fixture_purpose.label: Fixture Purpose
fixture_purpose.tooltip: What the light is used for — this will help determine the lumens/ft of the fixture.
fixture_purpose.learnMore: Accent picks out an object or edge (lower output). Task lights a work surface (medium output). Ambient fills a room (higher output).

installation_method.label: Installation Method
installation_method.tooltip: How the fixture mounts to the building.
installation_method.learnMore: Surface mounts onto a finished face. Recessed sits inside a routed channel. Angled aims light from a corner. Drywall / plaster-in is mudded flush for a trimless look. Suspended hangs from cables. This selects the profile family.

target_cct.label: Color Temperature (CCT)
target_cct.tooltip: Warmth of white light, measured in Kelvin (K).
target_cct.learnMore: 2700K is warm / residential, 3000K warm-neutral, 3500K neutral, 4000K cool / commercial. Tunable white products cover a range instead of one value.

cri.label: CRI (Color Rendering)
cri.tooltip: How accurately colors appear under the light, on a 0–100 scale.
cri.learnMore: 90+ is the standard for quality architectural lighting. 95+ is premium (galleries, retail, hospitality) but has limited availability in our current data — confirm before committing.

run_length.label: Run Length
run_length.tooltip: Total continuous length of the lit run, in feet.
run_length.learnMore: Long runs can drop voltage and exceed a single supply's capacity. We compare your length against the tape's max single-feed run length to flag when power injection or multiple supplies are needed.

power_injection.label: Power Injection
power_injection.tooltip: Feeding power at more than one point along a run.
power_injection.learnMore: When a run exceeds the max single-feed length, or total watts exceed one supply, you inject power mid-run or add supplies to keep brightness even end-to-end. This is derived for you from watts/ft × length vs. driver capacity.

supply_voltage.label: Supply Voltage
supply_voltage.tooltip: The input voltage feeding the tape or driver.
supply_voltage.learnMore: 24VDC is the common low-voltage tape standard. 120VAC line-voltage products have an integral or remote driver. The supply voltage must match the tape's input voltage and the driver's input type.

dimming_protocol.label: Dimming Protocol
dimming_protocol.tooltip: The control signal used to dim or color the fixture.
dimming_protocol.learnMore: 0-10V, TRIAC, ELV, and DALI dim white light. DMX512 and SPI drive color / pixels. The tape, driver, and controller must all speak the same protocol. Addressable pixel runs force DMX or SPI.

diffuser.label: Diffuser / Lens
diffuser.tooltip: The lens covering the LEDs — changes look and delivered brightness.
diffuser.learnMore: Clear is brightest but shows dots. Frosted hides dots with a small light loss. White diffuses fully (more loss, no hotspots). Black is a stealth daytime look. Lower transmission means fewer delivered lumens.

operating_temp.label: Operating Temperature
operating_temp.tooltip: The ambient temperature range at the install location.
operating_temp.learnMore: Extreme heat shortens LED life; extreme cold can affect some drivers. Tape operating temp is free-text in ERP, so this is optional and informational in the prototype.

finish.label: Finish
finish.tooltip: The visible color / finish of the extrusion.
finish.learnMore: Silver (mill / anodized), Black, and White are the common architectural finishes. Optional — it does not change the light, only the look of the channel.

lumens_per_foot.label: Lumens per Foot
lumens_per_foot.tooltip: Light output per foot of tape — a brightness measure.
lumens_per_foot.learnMore: Higher lumens/ft = brighter. Our purpose bands (accent / task / ambient) map onto lumens/ft ranges to rank products by how close they are to your goal.
